parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf10401-candidate`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Required Fields Present?

### Required Fields Present?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.email }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Check Calendar Availability · ②→ Abort — Malformed Input

### Abort — Malformed Input

Fails fast with a clear message; triggers the error workflow instead of processing bad data.

### Check Calendar Availability

googleCalendar — `getAll` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Slot Free? · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Slot Free?

Two-way gate on `={{ $items().length === 0 || !$json.id }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Create Calendar Event · ②→ Propose Alternative Slots

### Create Calendar Event

googleCalendar — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Confirmation (Microsoft Teams) · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Send Confirmation (Microsoft Teams)

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Hold Until Reminder Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Propose Alternative Slots

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Hold Until Reminder Time

Pauses 1 hours — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Send Reminder (Microsoft Teams)

### Send Reminder (Microsoft Teams)

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Enrich with Reference Data · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Slack · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Provision new customers with Jira and Microsoft Teams without manual work

**Pattern:** Onboarding Sequence · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Provision new customers with Jira and Microsoft Teams without manual work. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual onboarding is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Required Fields Present? → Check Calendar Availability → Slot Free? → Create Calendar Event → Send Confirmation (Microsoft Teams) → Hold Until Reminder Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to onboarding sequence without manual effort
- Anyone automating onboarding in human resources
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Improves response time
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your onboarding process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
